title: payment — trait composition
description: Five owned traits. `BelongsToPayment`, `BelongsToPaymentMethod`, `BelongsToPaymentIntent` are the FK traits for downstream models (PaymentDispute, PaymentIntent, PaymentMethod cross-references). `HasPaymentDisputes` + `HasPaymentMethods` are inverse-side conveniences on the Payment + Customer models. Order matters when composed with BelongsToTenant — tenant MUST come first so the scope chain observes the tenant context.

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `payment_id` FK. Adds the `payment()` belongsTo relation. Used by PaymentDispute — no downstream module currently attaches to payments via a payment_id FK held on this side (refund + chargeback are separate modules with their own FKs pointing at Payment).
usedBy: payment::PaymentDispute (required payment_id — every dispute anchored to exactly one payment)
composition_notes: Order: `BelongsToTenant` must appear BEFORE `BelongsToPayment` in the trait list so the tenant scope resolves first.
Refuses to save a row whose payment_id points at a payment in a different tenant than the row itself (defense-in-depth against cross-tenant FK smuggling).

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `payment_method_id` FK. Adds the `paymentMethod()` belongsTo relation. Used by Payment + PaymentIntent — the FK is nullable in both (some flows don't involve a saved method).
usedBy: payment::PaymentIntent (nullable payment_method_id — attached during requires_payment_method → requires_confirmation transition)

owned.description: Mixed into any domain model that carries a `payment_intent_id` FK. Adds the `paymentIntent()` belongsTo relation. Used by Payment — the intent is the parent for every payment.
usedBy: payment::Payment (required payment_intent_id — every payment born from a succeeded intent)

owned.description: Composed onto the Payment model. Adds `->disputes()` (hasMany over PaymentDispute), `->openDisputes()` (scope filter status NOT IN terminal), `->hasOpenDispute()` boolean helper, `->totalDisputedAmount()` sums amount_cents of open disputes.

Composed onto customer models (User, Athlete). Adds `->paymentMethods()` (morphMany over PaymentMethod via customer_type + customer_id), `->activePaymentMethods()` (scope filter is_active=true), `->defaultPaymentMethod()` (single is_default=true active method), `->cardPaymentMethods()` (scope filter method_type='card'). NOT installed by default — customer models opt-in via `use HasPaymentMethods` in their own class.
usedBy: user::User (opt-in — needs the composer to declare it)
composition_notes: This trait is meant to be composed onto MODELS OWNED BY OTHER MODULES (User, Athlete). The consumer includes `use HasPaymentMethods` — no automatic installation.

framework.reason: PaymentMethod, PaymentIntent, PaymentDispute. NOT Payment (immutable financial record — hard-purged by retention pipeline only).

foundation.reason: Payment (searchable by payment_number, provider_reference_id, invoice number cross-reference). NOT the other three (accessed via relations).

tenants.reason: Every owned row carries tenant_id per tenancy-columns.md §3.

audit.reason: Every write to payments / payment_methods / payment_intents / payment_disputes is compliance-relevant (financial retention 7 years; 10y Enterprise).

activity.reason: Payment + PaymentMethod + PaymentIntent + PaymentDispute — every admin action feeds the staff-visible activity feed.

payment.notes: Order matters: BelongsToTenant precedes every scope-observing trait. NO SoftDeletes — payments are immutable financial records; hard-purged by retention pipeline only. HasPaymentDisputes is inverse-side convenience.

payment_method.notes: SoftDeletes for the deactivate path (soft-delete + 90-day grace before hard-purge). Auditable is critical — provider_reference_id changes are compliance-relevant. NOT BelongsToPaymentIntent + NOT BelongsToPayment — methods are the PARENT of intents + payments (inverse relations).

payment_intent.notes: SoftDeletes for state-terminal intents in the 90-day retention window. Auditable + HasActivityLog because every state transition is worth surfacing.

payment_dispute.notes: SoftDeletes for retention-window handling (7y baseline; 10y Enterprise). Auditable + HasActivityLog because every dispute is compliance-sensitive.
